current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: The orthogonality regularization follow-up regressed on both proxy FVU and throughput, so more local regularization sweeps are not justified. This round adds a new `union_topk` family under `sparsify/` and tests it first at the same K=128/ef=8/Adam anchor, probing whether branch-level candidate union is a better way to add encoder expressivity than the recent additive, gating, or suppression mechanisms.

Stabilize the new union_topk family with a complementary-proposal encoder: the linear path still anchors selection, but the nonlinear branch now reads the linear residual and can surface extra candidates that the baseline top-k misses. This is a single quality-first proxy follow-up on the most recent new family, rather than another local sweep on hybrid_topk.

Pivot away from branch-union follow-ups and add a new refined_topk family. It preserves the baseline linear top-k proposal, but replaces the final coefficients with a ridge-refined fit on the chosen decoder features, targeting reconstruction quality directly rather than adding more routing machinery. This is a single proxy prototype for a more radical quality-first hypothesis at the K=128 anchor.

action.hypothesis: The recent hybrid and adaptive-threshold results suggest the main failure mode is not lack of an anchor but how nonlinear information is injected into support selection. A union_topk encoder that keeps the baseline linear top-k proposal intact while letting a nonlinear branch propose an alternate candidate set, with final support chosen from the union rather than from perturbed logits, could lower FVU by adding complementary features without destabilizing the proven linear path.
action.summary: The orthogonality regularization follow-up regressed on both proxy FVU and throughput, so more local regularization sweeps are not justified. This round adds a new `union_topk` family under `sparsify/` and tests it first at the same K=128/ef=8/Adam anchor, probing whether branch-level candidate union is a better way to add encoder expressivity than the recent additive, gating, or suppression mechanisms.
action.self_review: This is a single coherent architecture bet that directly addresses the weakness exposed by recent edits: nonlinear branches have either interfered with or failed to improve baseline support. The risk is that merged candidate sets may still be too redundant to beat hybrid_topk, but the design preserves the strongest prior while giving the nonlinear branch a cleaner path to help.
notes_to_memory: Round 16 adds a new union_topk family that preserves the linear TopK proposal and lets a nonlinear branch contribute only through alternate candidate proposals before the final global top-k.
A quick local import and forward check passed for union_topk before returning control to the runtime.
next_hypotheses: If union_topk shows a strong proxy gain, promote it to full as the next mainline quality candidate.

If union_topk is flat or weak, the next round should either stabilize this branch-union family with a family-specific width change or pivot to a more radical new encoder design rather than returning to recent discarded regularization ideas.

action.hypothesis: The union_topk prototype likely failed because its nonlinear branch proposed candidates from the same centered input as the linear branch, so it mostly duplicated baseline support. Conditioning the nonlinear proposer on the linear branch residual, widening that branch, and letting it over-propose candidates before the final global top-k should increase support complementarity at K=128 and lower proxy FVU.
action.summary: Stabilize the new union_topk family with a complementary-proposal encoder: the linear path still anchors selection, but the nonlinear branch now reads the linear residual and can surface extra candidates that the baseline top-k misses. This is a single quality-first proxy follow-up on the most recent new family, rather than another local sweep on hybrid_topk.
action.self_review: This stays within one coherent family hypothesis and directly targets the main weakness of the round-16 prototype: redundant candidate proposals. The main risk is that proxy training may still be too short for the wider residual-conditioned branch to separate from the linear anchor, but this is a more informative family-specific follow-up than pivoting away after one near-baseline prototype.
notes_to_memory: Round 17 stabilizes union_topk by making the nonlinear proposal branch residual-conditioned on the linear partial reconstruction instead of reading the same centered input.

The union_topk stabilization also widens the nonlinear branch and lets it over-propose 2k candidates before the final global top-k, aiming to improve candidate diversity without changing decode semantics.
A local import/forward/backward sanity check passed for the edited union_topk implementation before handing control back to the runtime.
next_hypotheses: If residual-conditioned union_topk materially improves proxy FVU, promote this family toward mainline comparison and consider a full run.

If it remains near the recent 0.275 proxy plateau under normal runtime, pivot away from branch-union variants to a more radical encoder family rather than spending more rounds on local union_topk tuning.

action.hypothesis: Recent architecture edits have mostly tried to improve support selection, yet multiple distinct families have converged to the same proxy FVU plateau under normal runtime. A stronger next bet is that support is not the main bottleneck at K=128; instead, the selected coefficients are too crude. A refined_topk family that keeps the proven linear top-k support but recomputes the chosen coefficients with a small per-example ridge solve against the decoder atoms could materially lower FVU by directly reducing reconstruction error on the selected support.
action.summary: Pivot away from branch-union follow-ups and add a new refined_topk family. It preserves the baseline linear top-k proposal, but replaces the final coefficients with a ridge-refined fit on the chosen decoder features, targeting reconstruction quality directly rather than adding more routing machinery. This is a single proxy prototype for a more radical quality-first hypothesis at the K=128 anchor.
action.self_review: This is a coherent architecture pivot after two flat union_topk rounds under normal runtime. The main risk is that the per-example solve may add overhead or overfit weak supports, but it tests a meaningfully different failure hypothesis than the recent support-selection families and keeps the launcher interface unchanged.
notes_to_memory: Round 18 adds a new refined_topk family that keeps the baseline linear top-k support but refits the selected coefficients with a small ridge solve against the decoder atoms.
The refined_topk implementation uses a straight-through blend so the forward path uses refined coefficients while preserving gradient flow through the baseline top-k activations.
A local import/forward/backward sanity check passed for refined_topk before handing control back to the runtime.
next_hypotheses: If refined_topk materially improves proxy FVU, follow with a family-specific stabilization pass on the coefficient refinement strength or support source before considering promotion.

If refined_topk is flat or regresses under normal runtime, that is evidence that coefficient refitting alone is not enough and the next round should pivot to a genuinely different encoder family rather than more top-k refinements.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
next_steps: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.

residual_topk.design_hypothesis: A residual two-branch encoder may reduce FVU at K=128 by letting the second encoder pass target what the first branch misses, which is directly aligned with the reconstruction objective. Test residual_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as a quality-first architecture probe.
next_steps: If residual_topk shows proxy improvement, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If residual_topk is also flat or worse, try factorized_topk next rather than returning to smaller-K search.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: The stabilized factorized_topk crash was caused by an implementation bug in the widened hidden-path projection, not by the architecture itself. Fix the hidden-to-latent projection and rerun the same K=128 factorized_topk stabilization pass to recover a valid quality signal.
next_steps: If the fixed factorized_topk now yields a valid proxy improvement, keep incubating the family with quality-focused tuning.
If the fixed factorized_topk still performs poorly under normal runtime, stop spending rounds on current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder design.

jumprelu.design_hypothesis: jumprelu looks promising enough to deserve a family-specific stabilization pass, and its current default threshold initialization is likely too weak to realize the benefit of learned gating. Test jumprelu at K=128 with Adam using a higher initial threshold and smoother STE bandwidth so the encoder starts from a more selective regime.
next_steps: If stabilized jumprelu materially improves proxy FVU, promote it to full before spending more rounds on hybrid variants.
If stabilized jumprelu is flat or worse, keep hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 as mainline and either try another new family or return to a training-dynamics experiment on the hybrid checkpoint.

gated.design_hypothesis: A gated SAE may materially lower proxy FVU at K=128 by decoupling support selection from coefficient magnitude, letting one branch learn which features should fire while another learns how strongly they should reconstruct. After two flat jumprelu stabilization rounds and stalled hybrid local search, this is the cleanest untried quality-first family already supported by the execution layer.
next_steps: If gated shows a strong proxy signal, promote it to full before returning to smaller-K or cost work.
If gated is also weak, bias the next round toward another genuinely different encoder family or a code edit for a new architecture rather than more parameter-only tuning on recent families.

adaptive_threshold_topk.design_hypothesis: The current best family may need cleaner support suppression rather than another additive or multiplicative residual path. An adaptive-threshold TopK encoder that keeps the proven linear logits as the anchor but learns an input-dependent nonnegative per-latent suppression term could reduce false activations at K=128 and materially improve FVU without abandoning the strong baseline initialization.
next_steps: If adaptive_threshold_topk shows a strong proxy gain, promote it to full as the next mainline quality candidate.

If adaptive_threshold_topk is flat or weak, the next round should bias toward a more expressive new architecture or a hybrid-family training-dynamics experiment rather than revisiting gated or jumprelu local sweeps.

union_topk.design_hypothesis: The union_topk prototype likely failed because its nonlinear branch proposed candidates from the same centered input as the linear branch, so it mostly duplicated baseline support. Conditioning the nonlinear proposer on the linear branch residual, widening that branch, and letting it over-propose candidates before the final global top-k should increase support complementarity at K=128 and lower proxy FVU.
next_steps: If residual-conditioned union_topk materially improves proxy FVU, promote this family toward mainline comparison and consider a full run.

If it remains near the recent 0.275 proxy plateau under normal runtime, pivot away from branch-union variants to a more radical encoder family rather than spending more rounds on local union_topk tuning.

refined_topk.design_hypothesis: Recent architecture edits have mostly tried to improve support selection, yet multiple distinct families have converged to the same proxy FVU plateau under normal runtime. A stronger next bet is that support is not the main bottleneck at K=128; instead, the selected coefficients are too crude. A refined_topk family that keeps the proven linear top-k support but recomputes the chosen coefficients with a small per-example ridge solve against the decoder atoms could materially lower FVU by directly reducing reconstruction error on the selected support.
next_steps: If refined_topk materially improves proxy FVU, follow with a family-specific stabilization pass on the coefficient refinement strength or support source before considering promotion.

If refined_topk is flat or regresses under normal runtime, that is evidence that coefficient refitting alone is not enough and the next round should pivot to a genuinely different encoder family rather than more top-k refinements.

next_move_guidance: If refined_topk materially improves proxy FVU, follow with a family-specific stabilization pass on the coefficient refinement strength or support source before considering promotion.

If refined_topk is flat or regresses under normal runtime, that is evidence that coefficient refitting alone is not enough and the next round should pivot to a genuinely different encoder family rather than more top-k refinements.
